game_name: The Simpsons: Bart vs. the Space Mutants

danger_signals.visual_form: Normal Springfield characters visually identical to humans until revealed
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy presence hidden in plain sight; danger is concealment

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual overlay/filter that exposes possessed characters
danger_signals.meaning: Reveals hidden threat; transforms innocent appearance into mutant identity

danger_signals.visual_form: Certain characters respond to player approach with hostile movement
danger_signals.meaning: Aggression indicator; distinguishes actual mutants from safe NPCs

danger_signals.visual_form: Purple-colored collectibles in Streets of Springfield
danger_signals.meaning: Alien weapon components; represent existential threat to world

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct enemy sprites with special animations
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat intensity; stage climax danger point

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible currency dropped by defeated mutants
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward resource accumulation; ability to purchase protective items

safety_signals.visual_form: Special item obtained from treasure containers
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety state; player becomes immune to harm
Extended survival window; reduced consequence of failure

safety_signals.visual_form: Rockets and cherry bombs acquired with coins
safety_signals.meaning: Tool availability for progression; agency to overcome obstacles

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible letters spelling family member names
safety_signals.meaning: Ally recruitment; narrative assurance of external support

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien possession effect; visible through X-Ray Specs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Disguised as normal Springfield citizens; appearance changes when possessed status revealed
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence throughout all five levels; embedded in civilian population across Springfield locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Letter collectibles spelling family member names
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Supportive NPCs appearing as allies during boss battles once letters are collected

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Normal human appearance; indistinguishable from mutants without X-Ray Specs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Some are possessed mutants; others are genuine innocent citizens
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban and commercial locations (streets, mall, amusement park, museum, power plant)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Space mutants disguised as normal characters throughout Springfield levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens behave normally until attacked; mutants display aggressive responses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level-specific collectible targets present (purple objects, hats, balloons, exit signs, nuclear rods)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mutants attempt to prevent Bart from collecting items; obstacles increase in complexity per level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: X-Ray Specs provide visual filter revealing mutant identities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains targeting advantage; mutants become distinguishable from innocents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Collected letters accumulate in inventory; visual progress toward family names
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Family members join boss battles once names are spelled; gameplay support increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environment changes (streets → mall → amusement park → museum → nuclear plant)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patterns increase in complexity; boss encounters become progressively harder

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Industrial setting representing existential threat location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ultimate confrontation with alien leadership; maximum difficulty state
Aliens gloat and mock Bart; stage-specific victory cinematics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: End scene showing enraged aliens vowing to leave Earth
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens attempt gracious defeat acknowledgment; aliens pay respects

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level represents key Springfield infrastructure (streets, commerce, entertainment, history, power) where mutant invasion is occurring; visual progression through civilization layers

environmental_storytelling.technique: Prank call mechanic as narrative revelation
environmental_storytelling.example: Calling Moe triggers character behavior change (bartender leaves post); reveals how Bart's knowledge enables manipulation of civilian actions against alien interests
Skateboard usable only in specific sections; indicates Bart's adaptation of tools to particular environmental contexts
Kwik-E-Mart sign interaction (shooting 'E' letter); shows how commercial landmarks become tools for revealing hidden mechanics
Collecting and destroying specific items (purple aprons, hats, balloons) prevents aliens from assembling weapon components; visual collection progress demonstrates sabotage effectiveness
Boss sprites larger and more elaborate than regular enemies; communicates escalating threat intensity at level boundaries

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves and acts like normal Springfield citizen; no visual distinction without X-Ray Specs
Becomes vulnerable to jumping attack; displays aggressive response patterns
Moves passively; no threat response to Bart's presence; cannot be defeated
Can identify and target mutants; gains combat advantage
Limited equipment access; increased danger from enemy encounters
Immune to harm; can move through dangerous areas safely
Appear as support during boss encounters; provide combat assistance
Display complex attack patterns; require equipment and family support to defeat

notes: The game's core manifestation mechanic is deception and revelation: mutants are visually indistinguishable from citizens until X-Ray Specs expose them, creating a 'hidden threat' paradigm where danger is concealment. World-state communication relies on environmental progression (civilization infrastructure) combined with item collection mechanics that signal sabotage of alien plans. The game employs a scaffolding narrative where family recruitment (letter collection) and equipment accumulation (coins → tools) visually demonstrate increasing player agency against the threat. The prank call system and commercial landmark interactions showcase how Bart's knowledge allows him to manipulate the normal world against alien interests, communicating that information asymmetry is the player's primary advantage.
